topic.title: SAP Event Management (SAP EM) / Event Tracking Integration

learningObjectives.title: Understand event management integration
learningObjectives.description: Explain how event systems provide real-time transportation visibility to SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand event sources
learningObjectives.description: Describe event capture from carriers, GPS, IoT, EDI, and SAP systems.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate TM and EM roles
learningObjectives.description: Clarify planning/execution ownership versus monitoring and alerting responsibilities.

sections.content: SAP Event Management (SAP EM), now commonly delivered through SAP Business Network for Logistics and Event Management capabilities, integrates with SAP Transportation Management to provide real-time shipment visibility. It collects transportation events from SAP systems, carriers, GPS devices and IoT sensors, allowing businesses to monitor transportation progress and respond proactively to exceptions.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP TM plans and executes transportation, while Event Management monitors what actually happens during transportation.

A refrigerated pharmaceutical shipment is monitored using GPS and temperature sensors. During transportation, the temperature exceeds the permitted range. SAP Event Management immediately receives the event, updates SAP TM, creates an exception alert and notifies the transportation planner to take corrective action.

sections.title: SAP TM vs SAP Event Management
Best Practice
sections.content: Integrate SAP TM with GPS providers, carrier portals and IoT devices to automate event capture instead of relying on manual shipment updates.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global food manufacturer integrated SAP TM with IoT temperature sensors and GPS tracking. Shipment events were captured automatically, enabling proactive intervention whenever cold-chain conditions were violated.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: SAP TM plans transportation, SAP Event Management monitors transportation. Event Management does not create Freight Orders.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Event Management is not a transportation planning tool. Its primary purpose is monitoring execution, tracking milestones and triggering alerts for exceptions.

items: SAP Event Management provides real-time shipment visibility.
Events originate from carriers, GPS, IoT devices and SAP systems.
SAP TM receives execution updates through integration.
Event Management supports proactive exception handling.
Integration improves shipment tracking and customer communication.
